intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

body.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

draft.title: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

Building your first draft…

data.intent: a personal dashboard for tracking long-term health experiments

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer a warm, conversational tone (like texting a health coach) over clinical language
facade.label: Casual checkups
facade.acceptImplies: Lean into conversational language, encouraging tone, approachable visual style
facade.rejectImplies: Shift toward clinical precision, formal terminology, data-forward design

evidence.hypothesis: Users prefer a warm, conversational tone (like texting a health coach) over clinical language
evidence.implication: Lean into conversational language, encouraging tone, approachable visual style

facade.hypothesis: Users building health dashboards prefer clean, data-focused visuals over decorative elements
facade.label: Minimal line charts
facade.acceptImplies: Lean toward sparse, functional charts with thin strokes and negative space
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward richer visual language—colors, icons, or more illustrative elements in data presentation

facade.hypothesis: Smooth transitions between data states feel more inviting than instant updates
facade.axisTargeted: Motion continuity — animated transitions vs. instant/snappy state changes
facade.label: Gentle fades
facade.acceptImplies: User expects fluid, eased animations when metrics update or sections load; prefers feeling of grace over speed
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers instant feedback and snappy state changes; animation feels like lag or unnecessary embellishment

facade.hypothesis: A fixed left sidebar with experiment sections (current, paused, completed) helps users navigate their dashboard without losing context
facade.axisTargeted: Layout structure — sidebar vs tabs for navigation
facade.label: Sidebar navigation
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers persistent navigation that keeps their mental model of experiments visible; explore density and card layouts next
facade.rejectImplies: User favors a cleaner, more minimal entry point; probe tab-based or floating navigation alternatives

facade.hypothesis: Users tracking long-term health experiments prefer soft, encouraging visual feedback (color, typography, micro-animations) over clinical, sterile data presentation
facade.label: Warm glow
facade.acceptImplies: Dashboard should use warm colors, rounded corners, celebratory language for progress, and gentle visual reward systems
facade.rejectImplies: Dashboard should prioritize neutral/cool aesthetics, sharp typography, and matter-of-fact data reporting without emotional design flourishes

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
axes.poleA: All at once or step by step?

axes.label: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal clinical aesthetics vs. warm, human-centered visual design for health data
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants data-dense dashboard layout vs. progressive disclosure with one metric at a time
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to motivational/encouraging tone vs. neutral, factual reporting voice
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants celebratory, gamified feedback vs. calm, meditative tracking environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers raw numbers and charts vs. narrative summaries and insights
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback on actions vs. slow, contemplative animations and transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: A warm, intimate mood with organic spacing and gentle typography will feel more personal and less clinical than hard-edged interfaces, making daily health tracking feel like self-care rather than medical reporting.
facade.axisTargeted: warm vs cool; organic vs digital
facade.label: Soft data cards
facade.acceptImplies: User gravitates toward human-centered, tactile design language—rounded corners, breathing room, approachable typography. Likely wants the dashboard to feel like a journal or companion, not a lab instrument.
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers clarity through structure and visual efficiency. May want more digital, minimal aesthetic—sharper lines, denser information layout, stronger visual hierarchy. Tracking should feel scientifically rigorous, not cozy.

facade.hypothesis: Users may resist a sterile, medical interface and prefer the conversational warmth already accepted over a traditional health-tracking aesthetic
facade.axisTargeted: Tone and visual personality — warmth vs clinical detachment
facade.label: Clinical dashboard
facade.acceptImplies: Double down on approachable, human-centered design; avoid medical/pharmaceutical visual language; use softer typography and encouraging microcopy
facade.rejectImplies: Users are willing to accept some clinical structure if it serves clarity; warmth preference isn't universal; consider hybrid approach with friendly tone layered over organized medical framework
